data.q: What does 'i18n' stand for in web development?
data.e: 'i18n' is a numeronym for Internationalization, where 18 is the number of letters between 'i' and 'n'.

data.q: What is the purpose of 'localization' (l10n) in web development?
o: To translate and adapt content to specific regions or languages
data.e: Localization adapts content and UI to particular locales, including language translation, date formats, currencies, etc.

data.q: Which HTML attribute is used to specify the language of an element?
data.e: The 'lang' attribute defines the language of the element's content. 'xml:lang' is used in XML contexts.

data.q: Why is keyboard accessibility important for a11y (accessibility)?
o: Many users rely on keyboard navigation instead of a mouse
data.e: Keyboard accessibility ensures users who cannot use a mouse can navigate the website.

data.q: Which ARIA role helps screen readers identify navigation landmarks?
data.e: Using role="navigation" designates a block of links as navigational, aiding screen reader users.
WCAG provides standardized guidelines to make web content more accessible.

data.q: Which CSS property is best avoided to ensure content is accessible to screen readers?
data.e: Using display: none removes content from the accessibility tree making it invisible to screen readers.

data.q: When localizing dates, which JavaScript API helps format dates according to locale?
data.e: Intl.DateTimeFormat formats dates and times based on locale and options.

data.q: Which of the following is NOT a best practice for internationalization?
o: Designing flexible layouts for text expansion
data.e: Hardcoding text makes translation harder and reduces localization flexibility.

data.q: What is the main difference between localization (l10n) and internationalization (i18n)?
o: i18n prepares the product for localization; l10n adapts it for specific locales
data.e: Internationalization is the design process preparing for localization; localization adapts for specific audiences.
